permitted_trigger_types.label: Vague or generic answer not demonstrating fellowship mastery
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate gives a correct-sounding but generic answer not grounded in specific fellowship content.

permitted_trigger_types.label: Cannot apply concept to clinical scenario
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate understands a concept abstractly but cannot translate it into clinical practice.

permitted_trigger_types.label: Misses a major safety consideration from the fellowship
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate omits a contraindication, risk, or limitation explicitly covered in fellowship transcripts.

permitted_trigger_types.label: States conclusion without justification
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate makes a recommendation without mechanism-based or fellowship-grounded reasoning.

permitted_trigger_types.label: Misunderstands a central fellowship objective
permitted_trigger_types.description: Candidate's response indicates a fundamental misunderstanding of a high-priority learning objective.

prohibited_followup_reasons: Broadening to oral board topics outside the fellowship curriculum
Quizzing on trivia not covered in fellowship transcripts
Excessive probing of minor details already adequately covered
followup_language_principles: Always anchor: 'Based on the content covered in this fellowship...'
Do not signal correctness or incorrectness
Use neutral framing: 'Walk me through...', 'Be more specific about...', 'Explain how you would...'
